channel.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India
channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: Indian-origin couple convicted in UK over Covid PPE fraud that funded £126k Porsche, Rolexes
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Australian agronomist Tony Rinaudo's FMNR revives Niger's dry landscapes with 200 million trees
item.description: An Australian agronomist's simple idea transformed Niger's dry landscapes into a success story. Tony Rinaudo helped farmers regenerate over 200 million trees by reviving existing root systems. This low-cost approach restored millions of hectares of degraded farmland and improved food security. Indigenous trees regenerated, providing shade, preventing soil erosion, and enriching the soil. The technique is now a global model for restoring degraded land worldwide.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Charles Darwin: "Ignorance more frequently begets confidence..."
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Meet Evan Budz: The 16-year-old Canadian who built an AI-powered robot turtle to detect microplastics with 94% accuracy
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Scientists revisit a forgotten Soviet plan to dam the Bering Strait and see if it could help protect Earth's climate
item.description: Soviet engineer Petr Borisov proposed damming the Bering Strait to warm Siberia. This bold plan aimed to transform frozen lands into productive agricultural areas. Modern researchers now study closing the strait to stabilize ocean circulation. Their simulations explore impacts on the Atlantic Meridional Overturning Circulation system. Scientists remain cautious about such large-scale environmental interventions.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Thousands of idle ships trapped near the Strait of Hormuz could become floating nurseries for invasive marine species
item.description: Ships lingering in the Persian Gulf could unintentionally facilitate the spread of harmful marine invaders worldwide. Extended anchorage allows marine organisms to thrive on the hulls, creating significant biofouling concerns that threaten global maritime commerce. These invasive species can inflict extensive ecological and economic harm on coastal areas. It is imperative for nations to join forces to address biofouling issues and prevent future outbreaks of invasion.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Orcas filmed tearing apart giant Sunfish with new hunting technique
item.description: Researchers have documented a previously unknown hunting behaviour in orcas in the Gulf of California. The animals were filmed working together to hold and ram giant sharp-tail sunfish until the carcass broke into thousands of pieces. Scientists believe the behaviour may help younger orcas feed more easily or could simply be play.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Tony Parker's mansion slashes price: Texas estate drops to $11 million; despite luxury features, still seeking buyer
item.description: Tony Parker's Texas mansion has seen another significant price reduction. The sprawling estate is now listed for eleven million dollars after several adjustments. This luxurious property features an enormous private water park and extensive sports facilities. Despite extensive publicity and a past peak price of twenty million dollars, it remains unsold. The highly customized luxury estate continues its search for a new owner.
item.dc:creator: TOI Real Estate Desk

item.title: Margot Robbie secretly splashes $28m on a Queensland ranch estate
item.description: Margot Robbie has made headlines with her recent acquisition of a lavish estate in Queensland, costing $28 million. Nestled in the scenic Currumbin Valley, this property holds significant nostalgia for the actress, as it's near where she grew up. After leaving the Gold Coast in her youth to chase her acting ambitions, this return represents a heartfelt reconnection with her origins during her flourishing career.
item.dc:creator: TOI Real Estate Desk

item.title: World's biggest marine mammal rescue begins in Canada as 30 beluga whales are relocated from shuttered Marineland after euthanasia fears
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: The Boomerang Nebula is three times colder than Antarctica, and colder than space itself
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Oak tree which survived World War II bomber crash, competing for Tree of the Year
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Climate change is driving beavers into Canada's Arctic, where they are reshaping the landscape
item.description: A new study has traced the northward expansion of North American beavers into Canada's Arctic by analysing shrub growth scars and satellite images. Researchers found evidence of colonisation beginning in 2008 and say the method can help track wildlife spread, landscape changes and the effects of climate change in remote Arctic regions.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Florida woman arrested after hoarding 80 cats and 12 dogs in a home with no AC, a foot of faeces and horrific living conditions
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: The world's largest waterfall isn't on land. It is hidden beneath the ocean, where billions of litres of water plunge every second
item.description: An immense underwater waterfall exists between Greenland and Iceland. Cold, dense seawater sinks beneath warmer Atlantic waters creating this cascade. This hidden giant plays a crucial role in ocean currents and global climate. Researchers use specialized instruments to monitor this vast, dynamic underwater system. Its flow influences heat and salt distribution across the Atlantic Ocean basin.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Scientists claim Moon is drifting away from Earth at 3.8 centimetres a year. What will happen to the planet as it goes further?
item.description: The Moon is gradually distancing itself from Earth, which has significant implications for our planet. Over millions of years, this migration will result in extended daylight hours and the end of total solar eclipses. Furthermore, the weakened tides will disrupt coastal ecosystems and marine life. As the Moon ascends to a higher orbit, Earth's rotation continues to decelerate, marking a slow but profound transformation in our natural environment.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Solar on stilts project: Philippines is building 422 MW of solar farms above fish ponds
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: “We’re told they…”: Candace Owens challenges Andrew Kolvet with ‘retraction’ demand in escalating Charlie Kirk feud
item.description: Candace Owens and TPUSA spokesman Andrew Kolvet publicly clashed after Kolvet questioned a Daily Mail report involving Erika Kirk. Owens responded by posting screenshots she said showed the newspaper requested comment before publication. Kolvet accused her of benefiting from the controversy, while Owens challenged TPUSA to seek legal action if the reported quotes were fabricated.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: US, Saudi Arabia sign civil nuclear deal paving way for uranium enrichment
item.description: The United States and Saudi Arabia signed a civil nuclear cooperation agreement. This pact allows for a joint study on uranium enrichment facilities. US officials stated the deal upholds safety and nonproliferation standards. Congress will review the agreement, facing potential opposition from lawmakers. The deal comes amid ongoing military operations against Iran.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: D4vd preliminary hearing: Celeste Rivas' family lawyer describes 'gruesome' crime scene photos
item.description: Singer D4vd's preliminary hearing revealed disturbing crime scene photos. Celeste Rivas' family lawyer described the evidence as gruesome and painful. Prosecutors allege D4vd lured Rivas to his home and dismembered her body. D4vd has pleaded not guilty to all charges against him. The case continues as the judge determines if it will proceed to trial.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy
